_doc: Universal/static text content for all 5 platform tabs. These texts are the same for every client.

why_we_look_at_this.title: Why We Look at This
why_we_look_at_this.text: Your gut microbiome is a complex ecosystem of trillions of bacteria that directly influences your digestion, immunity, energy levels, and overall wellbeing. By analyzing the DNA of bacteria in your stool sample, we can understand which bacteria are present, how they work together, and what metabolic functions they perform. This gives us a personalized map of your gut health that guides targeted, science-based recommendations.

processing_food_intro.title: How Your Gut Is Processing Food Right Now
processing_food_intro.text: These four metabolic 'dials' show how your bacteria are currently processing food and producing beneficial or harmful compounds. Together, they paint a picture of your gut's metabolic state — whether it's running on the right fuel, converting food efficiently, and maintaining a healthy balance.

why_this_matters.title: Why This Matters
why_this_matters.intro: Your test analyzes bacteria in your stool — a snapshot of what's leaving your system.

what_tells_us.title: What this tells us:
items: Which bacteria are present and how abundant

what_doesnt_tell_us.title: What it doesn't tell us:
items: How active bacteria are RIGHT NOW
Exact amounts of compounds being produced
What's happening in your small intestine or stomach
Whether patterns cause YOUR specific symptoms

why_this_matters.weather_analogy: Think of it like a weather report: Tells you today's conditions and helps predict what's coming, but not the complete climate story.

for_you.title: For YOU:
items: Changes take 3-7 days to show in stool (reflects recent past)
We combine this with your symptoms, diet, health history for complete picture
This shows optimisation opportunities, not diseases

intro.title: Your Gut Bacteria Groups
intro.text: Your gut bacteria don't work alone — they form specialized teams, each responsible for a different stage of food processing. We call these teams 'bacterial guilds.' By measuring how strong each team is, we can see where your gut is performing well and where it needs support. Think of it as a staffing report for your gut's workforce.

chain_description.title: How Bacteria Work Together as a Chain
chain_description.text: Your gut bacteria process food in stages, like an assembly line. Each team passes its work to the next. If one team is understaffed, the whole production line slows down. Here are the six teams we assess, in the order they work:

steps_summary.name: Fiber-Processing Bacteria
steps_summary.role: Break down plant foods into raw materials for other teams

steps_summary.name: Bifidobacteria
steps_summary.role: Convert sugars into lactate — fuel for downstream teams

steps_summary.name: Intermediate Processors
steps_summary.role: Bridge the gap between raw materials and finished products

steps_summary.name: Gut-Lining Energy Producers
steps_summary.role: Produce butyrate — the primary energy source for your gut lining

steps_summary.name: Protein-Fermenting Bacteria
steps_summary.role: Process proteins — ideally kept in check

steps_summary.name: Mucus-Layer Bacteria
steps_summary.role: Maintain your gut's protective mucus layer

bacterial_groups_tab.capacity_explanation: To help you understand each team's staffing level, we normalize all six teams to a scale of 100 ideal workers. This shows you at a glance how many workers each team has compared to its ideal size.

root_causes_tab.title: Underlying Causes of Imbalance
root_causes_tab.disclaimer: This is a behavioral inference from microbial patterns. It reflects what your bacteria suggest about recent conditions and should be confirmed with food diary data where available.

vitamins_tab.title: Vitamins
vitamins_tab.intro: Your gut bacteria produce certain vitamins in addition to what you get from food. This section checks whether your bacterial community has the capacity to support robust vitamin production, or whether dietary reinforcement may be needed.
functional_roles: Rapid gut lining renewal (3-5 day turnover)

folate.role: DNA synthesis, red blood cells, cell division

B_complex.role: Energy metabolism, brain function, nervous system support

biotin.role: DNA repair, cell growth, fatty acid synthesis

B12.role: Nerve function, red blood cell formation, DNA synthesis

action_plan_tab.title: Your Personalized Action Plan
action_plan_tab.intro: Based on your microbiome analysis, here is a prioritized intervention roadmap. Steps are ranked by their potential impact on your overall gut ecosystem efficiency.

timeline_estimates.fiber_degrader_expansion: Ongoing — gradual dietary and ecological shift

next_steps: If you have not yet completed the questionnaire, complete the dietary questionnaire for fine-tuned recommendations
Retest in 16 weeks to measure progress
action_plan_tab.reversibility_note: Microbiome imbalances are typically not permanent. With targeted intervention, significant improvement is expected within 12-16 weeks.
